architectural_purpose: deterministic orchestration topology visibility without graph execution
explicit_limitations: v4.3 Phase 2 creates deterministic orchestration topology visibility only.
v4.3 Phase 2 describes structural relationships before traversal.
v4.3 Phase 2 does not enable graph traversal execution.
v4.3 Phase 2 does not enable orchestration execution.
v4.3 Phase 2 does not enable runtime execution.
v4.3 Phase 2 does not enable routing execution.
v4.3 Phase 2 does not enable scheduling execution.
v4.3 Phase 2 does not enable sequencing execution.
v4.3 Phase 2 does not enable dependency resolution.
v4.3 Phase 2 does not enable topology repair or graph repair.
v4.3 Phase 2 does not enable orchestration inference.
v4.3 Phase 2 does not enable operational authorization or readiness approval.
v4.3 Phase 2 does not enable planner integration.
v4.3 Phase 2 does not enable production consumption.
v4.3 Phase 2 does not enable runtime or operational state mutation.
v4.3 Phase 2 does not enable ranking, scoring, selection, optimization, or recommendations.
explicit_prohibitions: No ranking, scoring, selection, or optimization exists.

diagnostics.finding: Topology identity is deterministic and non-executable.
Unsupported topology relationships remain visible and unavailable.
Blocked topology relationships remain visible and do not trigger traversal.
Prohibited topology relationships remain fail-visible and inactive.
Stale topology ancestry remains read-only evidence.
Conflicting authorization relationships remain blocked and descriptive.
Missing runtime execution metadata remains visible because execution is prohibited.
Topology explainability describes unavailable traversal, routing, dependency resolution, and execution.
Topology infrastructure remains non-executing, non-traversing, non-routing, and non-resolving.

explainability_summaries.summary: The topology is blocked for operational activation because Phase 2 models structure only.
Runtime execution relationships are prohibited by non-execution topology governance.
Future provider relationships are unsupported until a later approved governance phase.
Prior v4.2 coordination topology ancestry is stale read-only evidence, not a live source.
Execution authorization relationships conflict with the explicit non-execution boundary.
Topology traversal is unavailable because the topology is evidence, not an executable graph.
Routing is unavailable because relationship visibility does not select paths or routes.
Dependency resolution is unavailable because relationships are not resolvers.
Execution remains disabled across graph, runtime, routing, scheduling, and sequencing surfaces.

unsupported_reason_visibility: future provider topology contracts remain unsupported

blocked_reason_visibility: operational activation remains blocked by descriptive-only topology scope

stale_reason_visibility: v4.2 coordination topology ancestry is read-only evidence

conflicting_reason_visibility: execution authorization metadata conflicts with non-execution topology governance

missing_metadata_visibility: runtime execution policy metadata is intentionally absent because execution is prohibited

prohibited_reason_visibility: runtime orchestration execution relationships are prohibited
